key_themes: Strong Q3 2026 earnings beat and significantly raised FY2026 revenue/EPS guidance
Operational success with Network 2.0 cost savings and margin expansion
FedEx Freight spin-off on track for June 2026 with 12% operating margin target
Exceptional stock performance (36% YTD, 93% 1-yr) with bullish analyst consensus (~$403 avg target)
Resilient demand outlook despite some notes on rich valuation and insider selling
summary: FedEx reported a strong fiscal Q3 2026 beat with $5.25 adjusted EPS (vs ~$4.10 expected) and $24B revenue, prompting raised full-year guidance for 6-6.5% revenue growth and adjusted EPS of $19.30-20.10. The stock has rallied substantially to around $392 with a 36% YTD gain, supported by analyst Buy/Overweight ratings and an average price target of $403. The upcoming Freight spin-off is viewed positively with ambitious margin goals, though some social and Reddit chatter flags high valuations and recent insider sales as mild cautions amid otherwise bullish investor sentiment.
sources: 20+ news articles (CNBC, Reuters, Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Mar-Apr 2026), 30+ X posts analyzed (financial mentions largely positive; many non-financial), Multiple Reddit threads (r/Fedexers, r/Daytrading, r/options on earnings, spinoff, insiders)
